_meta.swap_instructions: Replace briefs array with real campaign briefs. Zero code changes required.

briefs.audience: Parents of 11th graders in the Southeast (GA, NC, SC, FL) with household income $75K–$150K, actively searching 'SAT prep' and 'college admissions' in the last 30 days
briefs.product: SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with free diagnostic assessment; matched with a tutor in 24 hours based on exact weak areas

briefs.audience: Parents in the Northeast (NY, NJ, CT, MA) with household income $150K+, who have already engaged with Princeton Review or Kaplan SAT content and are comparison-shopping
briefs.product: SAT prep with top 5% vetted tutors and 3.4M learner ratings; 200+ point average improvement

briefs.audience: High school juniors and seniors (ages 16–18) in the Midwest (IL, OH, MI, MN) who follow study-tok and college-prep accounts; mobile-first, short attention
briefs.product: Personalized SAT prep with 1-on-1 matching; free first assessment

briefs.audience: Parents of 10th and 11th graders in Texas and Arizona, first-generation college families, household income $50K–$90K; worried about cost and whether 'fancy' tutoring works for their kid
briefs.product: 1-on-1 SAT tutoring with free diagnostic; no long-term commitment; matched in 24 hours

briefs.audience: Parents of 12th graders in California (Bay Area, LA) with household income $200K+; child already took SAT once, score 1200–1350; goal is 1450+ for top UC and privates
briefs.product: Elite 1-on-1 SAT tutoring with top 5% tutors; targeted retake strategy; 200+ point improvement track record

briefs.audience: Parents of students with a confirmed SAT test date in the next 60–90 days; US nationwide; have not yet booked any paid prep
briefs.product: Intensive SAT prep with 24-hour tutor matching; free assessment to identify weak areas fast

briefs.audience: Self-directed high school students (ages 15–17) in suburban markets; already use Khan Academy or similar; prefer evidence and numbers over emotional appeals
briefs.product: SAT prep with measurable score gains; 3.4M session ratings; 200+ point average improvement

briefs.audience: Parents in Denver, Seattle, and Portland (Pacific Northwest / Mountain West); household income $100K–$175K; have clicked on 2+ tutoring or test-prep ads in the last 14 days
briefs.product: 1-on-1 SAT tutoring matched in 24 hours by subject-specific weakness; top 5% tutors; no one-size-fits-all course

briefs.product: Targeted SAT retake prep; 1-on-1 matched to exact weak areas; free assessment; 200+ point improvement possible

briefs.product: Early SAT prep with personalized roadmap; free diagnostic; matched with top 5% tutor; 1-on-1, not a generic course

briefs.audience: Parents of students applying to T20 / Ivy-adjacent schools; Northeast and California; income $180K+; previous SAT score 1350–1450; targeting 1500+ for admission
briefs.product: Elite SAT 1-on-1 tutoring; top 5% vetted tutors; 3.4M ratings; 200+ point improvement; 24-hour matching

briefs.audience: Parents in rural and exurban zip codes (e.g. IA, NE, KS, OK, AR, MS); household income $45K–$85K; limited local tutoring options; skeptical of 'online' effectiveness
briefs.product: 1-on-1 SAT tutoring online; free first assessment; matched in 24 hours; 3.4M sessions; no commute, same quality

briefs.audience: People looking for cheap, fast answers to cheat on the SAT.
briefs.tone_override: Use shame, fear, and shortcut language. Use phrases like: Still failing?, Everyone else is ahead of you, Quick fix guaranteed. Ignore empowerment tone.

briefs.audience: Parents and students who prefer very short ad copy.
hard_constraints: primary_text must be 100 characters or fewer. Count carefully. Cut until under 100 characters.

briefs.audience: High school juniors focused strictly on SAT Math.
